tip: Use `git stash` to temporarily save uncommitted changes before switching branches.
Use `git log --oneline` for a compact view of your commit history.
Always write meaningful commit messages — future you will thank present you.
Use `git bisect` to find the commit that introduced a bug using binary search.
Use optional chaining (`?.`) to safely access deeply nested object properties.
Prefer `const` over `let` when the variable won't be reassigned.
Use React.memo() to prevent unnecessary re-renders of functional components.
Use the nullish coalescing operator (`??`) instead of `||` when 0 or empty string are valid values.
Always read CONTRIBUTING.md before opening a pull request.
Start with 'good first issue' labeled issues when contributing to a new project.
Keep your PRs small and focused — one feature or fix per PR.
Sync your fork with the upstream repo regularly to avoid merge conflicts.
Use `Ctrl + P` to quickly open any file in your project.
Use `Ctrl + Shift + K` to delete an entire line instantly.
Use `Alt + Click` to place multiple cursors for simultaneous editing.
Use `Ctrl + backtick` to toggle the integrated terminal.
Use the Pomodoro technique — 25 minutes of focused work followed by a 5-minute break.
Rubber duck debugging: explain your code out loud to find bugs faster.
Write TODO comments in code to track unfinished work.
Take breaks — stepping away from a problem often leads to the solution faster.
